artifacts.title: Bird Info Dump
artifacts.body:
## Purpose

A compendium for organizing bird knowledge into readable sections.

This can eventually become an exportable book-like artifact.

artifacts.title: Minnesota Regions
artifacts.body:
## Minnesota Regions

There are several maps of Minnesota that break down each region. The map I found depicts the following titles for each region and helps understand both human and bird populace.

1. No Man's Land
2. God's Country
3. Southeast MN
4. Iron Range
5. North Shore
6. The Metro

artifacts.title: Birds of Minnesota
artifacts.body:
## Birds of Minnesota

Birds are among the most widespread and adaptable animals on Earth. They occupy forests, deserts, oceans, cities, and nearly every environment in between.

Their feathers, hollow bones, sharp senses, and varied behaviors have allowed them to evolve into thousands of unique species.

artifacts.title: Types of Birds
artifacts.body:
## Types of Birds

Birds are commonly grouped into broad categories based on their physical traits, habitats, feeding behaviors, and evolutionary relationships.

These categories include birds of prey, songbirds, waterfowl, woodpeckers, shorebirds, seabirds, and game birds.

artifacts.title: Great Ideas
artifacts.body:
## Purpose

A working compendium for collecting durable concepts and turning scattered notes into structured knowledge.

artifacts.title: What is an Idea?
artifacts.body:
## What is an Idea?

An idea is not just a note. It is a reusable unit of thought.

> A note captures something. An idea can be reused.
